description: Landing page copy evaluation rubric. 10 dimensions, weighted. Evaluates the text/copy layer, not visual design.

dimensions.name: Angle Clarity
dimensions.description: Is the strategic angle unmistakable from the headline and hero copy?

scoring_guide.1: No clear angle. Headline is generic. Could be any product or company.
scoring_guide.2: Angle is vaguely present but buried below the fold or muddled with competing themes.
scoring_guide.3: Angle is present in the hero but diluted by sections below the fold.
scoring_guide.4: Angle is clear and dominant from headline through final CTA. Minor distractions.
scoring_guide.5: Angle is unmistakable. Every section reinforces a single strategic position. Visitor could state the angle in one sentence.

dimensions.name: Motivation Match
dimensions.description: Does the page hit the intended buying motivation — the felt need, not just the stated need?

scoring_guide.1: Motivation is absent or wrong. The page speaks to a need the visitor does not feel.
scoring_guide.2: Motivation is present but superficial. Names the category without tapping the emotional driver.
scoring_guide.3: Motivation is addressed but at a rational level only. The felt need is implied, not activated.
scoring_guide.4: Motivation is clearly present and emotionally resonant. Visitor sees themselves.
scoring_guide.5: Motivation is deeply felt. The page articulates what the visitor feels but hasn't put into words.

dimensions.name: Tactic Execution
dimensions.description: Does the page structure (hero → proof → CTA) flow logically? Does each section build on the last?

scoring_guide.1: No recognisable structure. Page is a random collection of sections with no narrative arc.
scoring_guide.2: Structure attempted but breaks down. Sections feel disconnected or in wrong order.
scoring_guide.3: Structure present and mostly logical, but one section feels forced or out of place.
scoring_guide.4: Well-executed. Hero, proof sections, and CTA form a coherent persuasion arc.
scoring_guide.5: Flawlessly executed. Each section builds on the last. Scroll feels inevitable.

dimensions.name: Hero Clarity
dimensions.description: Can a visitor understand what this is, who it's for, and what to do within 5 seconds of landing?

scoring_guide.1: Visitor has no idea what this is after reading the hero. Headline is clever but unclear.
scoring_guide.2: Vaguely clear but requires reading the subhead and hero copy to understand.
scoring_guide.3: Clear enough. Headline communicates the category. Subhead adds specificity.
scoring_guide.4: Very clear. Headline + subhead communicate what, who, and why. CTA is visible.
scoring_guide.5: Instant clarity. What it is, who it's for, and what to do are all obvious from the headline alone. Zero ambiguity.

deterministic_rules.cta_above_fold: Primary CTA must be visible in hero section

dimensions.name: Proof Density
dimensions.description: Does the page stack enough credible proof to overcome skepticism? Stats, testimonials, logos, specifics.

scoring_guide.1: No proof. All claims are unsupported. Pure assertion.
scoring_guide.2: One proof element present (a testimonial or stat) but feels thin.
scoring_guide.3: Multiple proof types present. Some claims remain unsupported.
scoring_guide.4: Strong proof density. Stats, testimonials, and specifics reinforce each other.
scoring_guide.5: Overwhelming proof. Every claim is backed. Multiple proof types (social, statistical, narrative) create layered credibility.

dimensions.name: CTA Prominence & Clarity
dimensions.description: Is the primary CTA obvious, repeated at key moments, and compelling?

scoring_guide.1: No CTA, or CTA is buried at the very bottom. Visitor doesn't know what to do.
scoring_guide.2: CTA exists but only appears once and is easy to miss.
scoring_guide.3: CTA is clear and appears 2+ times. But text is generic ('Submit', 'Sign Up').
scoring_guide.4: CTA is compelling, appears at key scroll points, and is visually prominent.
scoring_guide.5: CTA is irresistible. Appears at hero, mid-page, and footer. Action-specific text. Reader always knows the next step.

deterministic_rules.cta_in_hero: Must be present above the fold

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.description: Concrete numbers, names, and details — not vague marketing speak.

scoring_guide.1: Entirely vague. No numbers, no concrete details. Pure marketing fluff.
scoring_guide.2: One concrete detail present but surrounded by vague claims.
scoring_guide.3: Multiple concrete details present. Some claims remain vague.
scoring_guide.4: Consistently specific throughout. Numbers are verifiable.
scoring_guide.5: Every claim is concrete and fact-checked. Specific costs, counts, timeframes, and outcomes.

dimensions.name: Scroll Depth Pull
dimensions.description: Does each section create enough curiosity or value to pull the reader to the next section?

scoring_guide.1: Page front-loads everything. No reason to scroll past the hero.
scoring_guide.2: Some sections add value but the page loses momentum mid-way.
scoring_guide.3: Reasonable flow. Each section adds something but transitions are weak.
scoring_guide.4: Strong pull. Section headings create curiosity. New proof/value at each scroll point.
scoring_guide.5: Exceptional pull. Each section ends with a hook for the next. Progressive disclosure builds to an inevitable CTA.

deterministic_rules.section_headings: Each section must have a clear, benefit-oriented heading

dimensions.name: Objection Pre-emption
dimensions.description: Does the page anticipate and address the top blockers before the reader raises them?

scoring_guide.1: No objections addressed. The most obvious 'but...' is left unanswered.
scoring_guide.2: Objection is weakly hinted at but not directly addressed.
scoring_guide.3: Primary objection is addressed but the counter-argument is vague.
scoring_guide.4: Primary objection is clearly pre-empted with specific proof.
scoring_guide.5: Top objection is pre-empted convincingly, and secondary objections are also addressed.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.description: Is this variant distinct from other page variants? Does it test a genuinely different hypothesis?

scoring_guide.1: Duplicate of another variant. Same headline, same structure, same proof.
scoring_guide.2: Surface-level differences only (different headline, same argument and flow).
scoring_guide.3: Different hero or structure but core persuasion logic overlaps with another variant.
scoring_guide.4: Clearly distinct. A visitor seeing both pages would recognize different approaches.
scoring_guide.5: Completely unique hypothesis. Different emotional entry point, different proof stack, different structure.
